failure_modes.detail: The Phase 4 signoff memo itself says final validation should use at least one real candidate-to-alert run rather than seeded or purely local test data.
failure_modes.title: Alert-loop claims without real traffic would be overstated

failure_modes.detail: Phase 6 reporting explicitly says threshold recommendations should stay shadow-only until they survive larger replay windows.
failure_modes.title: ML thresholds are shadow-only recommendations

failure_modes.detail: Phase 9 now demonstrates replay-to-alert reproducibility locally, but the packet still depends on seeded detector-input sources and noop evidence providers.
failure_modes.title: Replay-to-alert reproducibility is now demonstrated only on a seeded local packet

observability_caveats.detail: Phase 7 observability logic is implemented, but the current local alert/evidence history is too small to turn those metrics into strong measured conclusions.
observability_caveats.title: Goodhart and observability analysis still outpaces the local evidence base

observability_caveats.detail: The committed Phase 7 observability study code explicitly warns that better visible precision is not the same as preserved tradable or investigative edge.
observability_caveats.title: Visible operator metrics may not preserve true lead time or hidden candidate edge

provider_issues.detail: Current default evidence providers are ['noop_news', 'noop_social'], and the materialized Phase 9 packet used noop adapters only, so richer real retrieval evidence is still not demonstrated.
provider_issues.title: Evidence providers default to placeholder noop adapters

provider_issues.detail: Telegram and Discord integrations exist, but the canonical Task 2 packet recorded skipped rather than sent attempts because credentials were not enabled in the current environment.
provider_issues.title: Live delivery integrations are gated and may be disabled

weak_regimes.detail: Phase 9 materially populated the end-to-end packet, but the canonical reference window still covers only two alerts and two paper trades in a seeded local scenario.
weak_regimes.title: Materialized later-phase evidence is still tiny and seeded

weak_regimes.detail: Alert precision, calibration, lead time, and paper-trade edge can now be computed honestly from local artifacts, but they are still too small or too synthetic for strong deployment claims.
weak_regimes.title: Canonical v1 metrics are now available, but mostly descriptive rather than defensible

weak_regimes.detail: The committed Phase 6 trainer now supports LightGBM and the Task 4 artifact contract is satisfied, but the local evidence remains train-only on a two-row dataset.
weak_regimes.title: Boosted-tree ML exists, but held-out evidence is still missing

blockers: alert packet is only two alerts, so usefulness evidence is descriptive rather than statistically strong
Phase 4 evidence providers in the canonical local packet are noop adapters rather than real-provider-backed retrieval
delivery attempts were persisted, but outbound channels were skipped in the current local environment

evidence_sources.note: Defines the success-metric order and stop conditions.
Documents known Phase 4 limitations, especially placeholder providers and the need for stronger real-provider-backed evidence.
Current end-to-end freeze status and database table counts.
Phase 9 Task 2 evidence that candidate, alert, delivery, and analyst-feedback rows now exist locally.
Phase 9 Task 3 holdout-validation report for the canonical reference window.

metrics_bundle.evidence_summary: The workspace now contains a replay-linked local alert packet with persisted alerts, delivery attempts, and one analyst-feedback row. Current alert usefulness precision is `0.5` on a two-alert seeded packet, with delivery summary `sent=0 skipped=4`. This is enough to show the loop exists, but not enough to claim robust real-world precision.
metrics_bundle.metric_name: Ranked alert precision and operational usefulness
metrics_bundle.target: Precision@10 > 0.60 (aspirational), plus meaningful analyst usefulness evidence

blockers: the local dataset is only two labeled rows and uses train-only evidence
held-out baseline-beating evidence is not yet available
Phase 6 remains shadow-only in canonical v1 even after artifact completion

evidence_sources.note: Defines the success-metric order and stop conditions.
Contains calibration/reporting logic and explicit caveats about shadow-only threshold use and descriptive-only evidence.
Summarizes baseline margin and calibration profile status when evaluation runs exist.
Current end-to-end freeze status and database table counts.
Canonical v1 operating-mode decision used to interpret shadow ML versus authoritative logic.
Defines default evidence providers and precision target thresholds.
Phase 9 Task 4 summary of the LightGBM shadow artifact, required baselines, calibration, registry state, and shadow scores.

metrics_bundle.evidence_summary: The workspace now contains model evaluation rows, calibration profiles, a registered LightGBM shadow model, and shadow scores for the canonical window. The current required-baseline assessment is `descriptive_only_train_split`, which means the artifact contract is satisfied but the local evidence is still descriptive and not held-out-defendable.
metrics_bundle.target: Brier score < 0.20 (aspirational), with Phase 6 shadow thresholds currently targeting WATCH 0.55, ACTIONABLE 0.65, CRITICAL 0.80 precision slices

blockers: lead-time evidence comes from a seeded local packet with only one successful alert
evidence providers remain noop-backed, so corroboration timing is not representative of real retrieval behavior
Phase 7 observability analysis still lacks broader alert history beyond the tiny canonical packet

evidence_sources.note: Defines the success-metric order and stop conditions.
Defines measurable observability risks and deployment implications.
Current end-to-end freeze status and database table counts.
Phase 9 Task 2 evidence that candidate, alert, delivery, and analyst-feedback rows now exist locally.
Phase 9 Task 3 holdout-validation report for the canonical reference window.

metrics_bundle.evidence_summary: Lead-time analysis is now materialized for the canonical local packet. The current median lead time is `9495.0` seconds, which exceeds the 30-minute aspirational target on the single successful alert in the seeded packet. This demonstrates the reporting path, but the sample is too small and synthetic to treat as a strong production claim.
metrics_bundle.target: Median lead time > 30 minutes on the subset where corroboration exists

blockers: paper-trading evidence is based on only two trades
the current result is descriptive and vulnerable to sample-size noise
the strongest and weakest windows are the same single fixture packet

evidence_sources.note: Defines the success-metric order and stop conditions.
Defines replay, validation, and conservative paper-trading requirements.
Current end-to-end freeze status and database table counts.
Phase 9 Task 3 holdout-validation report for the canonical reference window.
Phase 9 Task 3 conservative paper-trading and backtest report for the canonical reference window.

metrics_bundle.evidence_summary: The conservative paper-trading framework is now materially populated for the canonical window. The current backtest packet shows median bounded PnL `18.63986` across `2` paper trades after explicit conservative assumptions. This is enough to prove the workflow and artifact contract, but not enough for a broad edge claim.
metrics_bundle.target: Positive paper-trade edge after fees and slippage

readiness_summary.highest_priority_gap: The project now has a materially populated end-to-end local packet, but the remaining blocker is stronger real-provider-backed and held-out-sized evidence rather than missing artifacts.

stop_conditions.reason: The canonical local packet now includes raw-archive and detector-input manifest rows for the frozen hour, so the stop condition is not triggered on the seeded packet itself.

stop_conditions.condition: duplicate trade inflation cannot be controlled
stop_conditions.reason: The canonical seeded packet replays deterministically and produces stable downstream counts, so this stop condition is not currently active on the local proof packet.

stop_conditions.condition: alert false-positive rate remains operationally unusable after suppression tuning
stop_conditions.reason: Local alert, delivery, and analyst-feedback rows now exist, but the packet is too small and synthetic to defend operational false-positive behavior for real deployment.

stop_conditions.condition: evidence-provider spend exceeds budget without measurable lift
stop_conditions.reason: Default providers are noop adapters and no live spend evidence exists locally, so overspend is not currently happening here, but real-provider lift remains unevidenced.

stop_conditions.condition: replay cannot reproduce a historical alert end to end
stop_conditions.reason: Phase 9 now reproduces a full local replay-to-alert-to-validation-to-ML packet for the canonical hour, but the evidence path is still seeded and noop-provider-backed rather than fully real-provider-backed.
